titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Howard Roberts Lamar Papers
titlestmt.author: by Susan Brady

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 9373823.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday June 18, 2022 at 03:05

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.physdesc: (152 boxes) + 2 broadside folders, 68 film reels
did.abstract: The Howard Roberts Lamar papers document the research, writing, teaching, and administrative activities of Yale history professor Howard Lamar from the 1940s through 2000. Included in the papers are manuscript drafts of books, articles, speeches, and book reviews; lecture notes for the history courses he taught at Yale and other universities; topical and personal files; and correspondence. Project proposals, scripts, research material, production files, photographs, and films document the "Westering" film project which Lamar co-developed in the 1970s.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Howard Roberts Lamar, 1994, 2002, 2010, 2012, and 2019, and Yale University Press, 1957.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Cold Storage 31-98 (film reels): Restricted fragile material. Consult the Beinecke Library Access Services department for additional information.
Boxes 20-21 and 109-119: Restricted until 2035. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Box 22: Restricted until 2064. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Box 120: Restricted until 2020. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Boxes 121-122: Restricted until 2075. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Box 123 (computer disks): Restricted fragile material. Copies of electronic files disks #4-21 from Box 99 are available through Access Services.
Box 124 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 221(audiovisual material and computer media): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 192: Restricted until 2039. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Howard Roberts Lamar Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Howard Roberts Lamar Papers. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received preliminary processing at the time of acquisition in 1957, 1994, and 2002. Further organization, rehousing and/or description was carried out in 2010. The collection is comprised of material formerly classed as Uncat WA MS Lamar, Uncat WA MS 177, and Uncat MSS 403. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below, according to month and year of acquisition.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

bioghist.head: Howard Roberts Lamar, 1923-
bioghist.p: Howard Roberts Lamar, historian of the trans-Mississippi West, was born in Tuskegee, Alabama in 1923. He received a B.A. from Emory University in 1944, and a Masters (1945) and Ph.d. (1951) in History from Yale University. He was an instructor at the University of Massachusetts and Wesleyan University between 1945 and 1949, and taught at Yale from 1949 to 1994. He held a number of administrative positions at Yale including chair of the Department of History 1962-1963 and 1967-1970, Dean of the College 1979-1985, and President of the University 1992-1993. He is Sterling Professor Emeritus of American History at Yale.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Howard Roberts Lamar papers document the research, writing, teaching, and administrative activities of Yale history professor Howard Lamar from the 1940s through 2010s. Included in the papers are manuscript drafts of books, articles, speeches, and book reviews; lecture notes for history courses he taught at Yale and other universities; topical and personal files; and correspondence. Project proposals, scripts, research material, production files, photographs, and films document the "Westering" film project which Lamar co-developed in the 1970s.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is organized into seven groupings: I. February 1957 acquisition, 1956. II. June 1994 acquisition, 1942-1993. III. July 2002 acquisition, 1969-1979. IV. August 2010 acquisition, 1942-2000. V. December 2010 acquisition, 1985 - 1995. VI. June 2012 acquisition, 2000 - 2011. VII. January 2019 acquisition, 1911-2018.

did.physdesc: (1 box)

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Formerly classed as Uncat WA MS Lamar.

did.unittitle: Dakota Territory, 1861-1889: A Study of Frontier Politics , typescript

did.physdesc: (22 boxes)

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Formerly classed as Uncat WA MS 177.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in five series: Writings, Lecture Notes, Topical Files, Personal Files, and Publications.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of various typescripts of books, articles and papers, speeches, book reviews; files relating to professional projects; class and lecture notes from courses both studied and taught by Lamar; correspondence, and miscellaneous publications.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes manuscripts of books, articles and papers, speeches, and book reviews by Howard Lamar.

did.unittitle: Dakota Territory, 1861-1889; a Study of Frontier Politics , typescript draft

title.part: The Far Southwest, 1846-1912: a Territorial History
The Frontier in History: North America and Southern Africa Compared

did.unittitle: Gold Seeker: Adventures of a Belgian Argonaut During the Gold Rush Years , by Jean-Nicolas Perlot; edited by Howard R. Lamar
"Jefferson, France, and Napoleon" (not published), drafts and outlines
Odds and Ends; Or, Incidents of a Cruise in the Pacific in the U.S. Ship Portsmouth, by Fore Peak, Pseudonym for Joseph T. Downey, 1845-1848 , typescript drafts

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Edited by Howard Lamar and published as The Cruise of the Portsmouth , 1845-1847)

did.unittitle: U.S. chapters for John W. Hall's world history volume, typescript drafts and notes
"Colorado: The Centennial State in the Bicentennial Year"
"Persistent Frontier: The West in the Twentieth Century"
"Public Values and Private Dreams: South Dakota's Search for Identity, 1850-1900"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes outlines, notes, and other materials for classes Lamar taught at Yale and other universities.

did.unittitle: "The New Freedom," first (guest) lecture at Yale
History, M.A. in Teaching (M.A.T.) program, Yale Summer Term
History, U.S. Army Specialized Reserve Training Program, University of Massachusetts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes reports, meeting minutes, and other material relating to Lamar's activities at Yale and other institutions.

did.unittitle: Report on Graduate History Program, University of Montana
Review of summer seminar, W.T. Jackson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of notes, papers, and other material relating to Lamar's undergraduate studies at Emory University, a few calendars from his years as an administrator at Yale, and correspondence.

did.unittitle: Undergraduate class notes, essays and related material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of books, periodicals, brochures, pamphlets, and offprints not written by Lamar.

did.unittitle: Bennington College: In the Beginning , by Thomas P. Brockway
Proceedings of the American Antiquarian Society , Vol. 87, Part 2
Yale Alumni Magazine , Vol. XLVII, no. 8
Yale Weekly Bulletin and Calendar (various issues)

did.physdesc: (7 boxes) + 1 broadside folder, 68 film reels

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of correspondence, project proposals, research files, scripts, film production files, photographs, films and other materials documenting the "Westering" film project.

did.physdesc: (25 boxes) + 1 broadside folder

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of writings, lecture notes, correspondence, computer disks, research, and personal files, and material relating to the Westering project.

scopecontent.p: Three drafts of speech.

Yale New Haven Teachers Institute Seminar on Regions and Regionalism

did.physdesc: (1 box)
5 folders

did.unittitle: Deanship of Yale College: a brief history
Reprints of Howard Lamar West article

did.physdesc: (96 boxes)

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 221 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of audiovisual material may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 222 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into nine series: I. Writings, 1911-2010; II. Correspondence, 1946-2018; III. Teaching papers, 1929-2008; IV. Diaries, calendars, and miscellaneous personal papers, 1937-2013; V. Writings and papers of others, 1971-2014; VI. Research materials, 1979-2012; VII. Papers related to professional organizations, 1973-2010; VIII. Photographs, 1985-2006; and IX. Audiovisual materials, undated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings, essays, books, teaching papers, correspondence, research materials, diaries, calendars, photographs, computer media, and audiocassettes of Howard Lamar and others.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 221 (computer media): Restricted fragile. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two sub-series: Books and major projects, 1955-2008 and Essays, lectures, and speeches, 1911-2020.

scopecontent.p: Includes essays, speeches, and lectures outside of Yale University by Howard Lamar.

The Western Historical Quarterly , quarterly publication
The Western Historical Quarterly , quarterly publication
"Women and the West: Rethinking the Western History Survey Course", essay
Writing the History of the American West , book
"A Long Way from the East", typescript copies and research

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes research notebooks from Howard Lamar's time as a university student and his own teaching and lecture material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Howard Lamar's personal diaries, calendars, correspondence related to his college dean and Yale presidency appointments, portrait material, computer media and photographs, art work, trip material, and notebooks from his time as a Yale University student.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Subfiles are arranged in chronological or alphabetical order.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 221 (computer media): Restricted fragile. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes research materials, speeches, photographic prints, computer media, and correspondence related to the Grove Street Cemetary and the 250th anniversary of Noah Webster's birth.
